Title: Synthetic bacterium-facilitated colonization of nitrogen-fixing bacteria for remodeling the rhizosphere microbiome and improving plant yield

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Artificial polysaccharide-binding fusion protein containing CBM_CipC domains and mCherry
Common_Genes.Source_Species: Synthetic gene optimized for E. coli; binding domain derived from Clostridium cellulolyticum CipC
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports plant growth, chlorophyll accumulation, biomass increase, and soil fertility
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Rhizosphere soil; plant roots and leaves indirectly enriched in N
Common_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Cell surface of engineered E. coli (surface-displayed protein)
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced in engineered E. coli by IPTG
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: Nitrogen-fixing bacteria surface polysaccharides; root surface polysaccharides
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: 16S rDNA amplicon sequencing; ITS amplicon sequencing; metabolomics
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Co-inoculation with Sinorhizobium medicae NKN632 and Sphingomonas endophytica NKN643 under low-nitrogen growth conditions
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Engineered expression in EcCMC; confocal microscopy with FITC-labeled polysaccharides and FITC-ConA; PCC co-localization analysis; root binding CFU assays; SEM colonization assays; rhizosphere microbiome profiling; metabolomics; soil nitrogenase/TN/SOM measurements
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes root recruitment and colonization of exogenous nitrogen-fixing bacteria, increasing rhizosphere nitrogen fixation, soil total nitrogen, soil organic matter, and plant growth/yield
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Cmc-expressing EcCMC specifically bound polysaccharides from nitrogen-fixing bacteria and root surfaces. 2. EcCMC acted as a physical bridge that markedly increased NFB root binding and colonization compared with EcM. 3. Co-inoculation of EcCMC with NFBs increased Rhizobiales and Sphingomonadales abundance, elevated nitrogenase activity and total soil nitrogen, and improved growth of Astragalus sinicus and Medicago sativa.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Potential ecological risk discussed as low because EcCMC was reportedly not detectable after 14 days in prior work; no growth penalty reported
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful synthetic biology tool to recruit beneficial microbes and improve biological nitrogen fixation, soil fertility, and legume yield; may serve as a universal microbiome-engineering bridge
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Classified as Common Gene because its main role is microbial recruitment/bridging rather than catalysis or transcriptional regulation
